expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: result.md includes a dedicated section: "## Case focus coverage: context intake preserves constraints & expectations" and explicitly maps all three sub-points: "1) Component-stack constraints are preserved", "2) Embedding lifecycle assumptions are protected at intake (no premature analysis)", and "3) Integration-sensitive customer expectations are captured as context signals" (including fixture evidence: "Customer reference: CVS Pharmacy, Inc. – CS0928640").

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: result.md states Phase 1 scope and constraints: "Phase 1 is responsible for context intake routing, not authoring QA content" and provides a "Phase alignment check (primary phase = phase1)" noting "No Phase 2+ artifacts are required or implied". execution_notes.md also frames evaluation as limited to "contract alignment" due to missing runtime artifacts, consistent with phase1 contract focus.
